items.title: Microsoft warns users of 'Crypto Clipper' malware spread via USB drives
items.summary: The malware blends data theft with remote code execution, “turning a financially motivated stealer into a lightweight backdoor,” Microsoft said.

items.title: Aster popped over 10% on radical 'buyback and burn' upgrade. But gains were short-lived
items.summary: ASTER sees two-way volatility as bullish protocol-specific news meets hawkish Fed.

items.title: Approval Phishing: From Just One Case to Full-Scale Disruption
items.summary: Chain of Thought is our new expert-hosted webinar series, taking you behind the scenes of real investigations, emerging typologies and… The post Approval Phishing: From Just One Case to Full-Scale Disruption appeared first on Chainalysis.

items.title: Bitcoin falls below $63,000 as risk assets sell off and the week's bounce fades
items.summary: Crypto dropped across the board on Friday in holiday-thinned trading, giving back the week's gains. With oil down 9% and the Iran deal signed, the question turns to whether this cycle gets an altseason at all.

items.title: Ireland mulls crypto safeguards in response to financial risks
items.summary: For the first time in seven years, the Irish government released an assessment related to digital assets, noting risks from money laundering, terrorism financing, sanctions violations and bribery.

items.title: Bitcoin’s deeply discounted versus AI-stocks, but hawkish Fed risk lingers: Bitwise
items.summary: Bitwise analysts say Bitcoin trades in a historical value zone, but hawkish Federal Reserve signals and a competition for liquidity could sideline buyers.

items.title: CME Group sues CFTC over crypto perpetual futures
items.summary: The lawsuit targets the federal regulator and Chair Michael Selig, alleging that the Commodity Futures Trading Commissioin is treating cryptocurrency “futures” as “swaps,” posing risks to derivatives markets.

items.title: What happens when ChatGPT becomes the front door to crypto
items.summary: ChatGPT could become crypto’s new onboarding gateway, making Bitcoin and wallets easier to use while creating new trust risks.

items.title: Microsoft found malware that hijacks crypto wallets and spreads through USB sticks
items.summary: The software intercepts shortcut files and directs them to install a worm that harvests private keys from the Windows clipboard and inserts its own destination wallet addresses when it detects a transfer.

items.title: XRP falls 3% after losing $1.15 support as breakout attempt fades
items.summary: Heavy selling pushed XRP back below a key support zone, reinforcing a downtrend that has repeatedly stalled rallies near $1.25.

items.title: Live markets: Bitcoin has traded below its mining cost for five months, squeezing miners
items.summary: About 20% of miners are now unprofitable, and publicly traded miners sold more than 32,000 bitcoin in the first quarter to cover operating costs, more than they offloaded in all of 2025.

items.title: Bitcoin traders load up on bearish bets all the way down to $52,000
items.summary: Bitcoin traders are scrambling to buy options bets that would pay off if the selloff deepens.

items.title: Goldman Sachs cuts year-end gold target by $500, doubting rate cuts
items.summary: Goldman Sachs revised its year-end forecast for gold to $4,900, indicating an increase from current levels, but less than previously expected.

items.title: AllUnity debuts SEKAU, a fully reserved Swedish krona stablecoin
items.summary: AllUnity launches Swedish krona-backed stablecoin SEKAU with multi-chain support, expanding its stablecoin portfolio regulated under the EU’s MiCA framework.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.summary: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Algorand plans ‘broad quantum resilience’ by 2027
items.summary: Algorand is planning new accounts and consensus mechanisms designed to be resistant to the cryptography-breaking threat of quantum computers.

items.title: US crypto ETFs are pulling Bitcoiners into TradFi: BlackRock's Jay Jacobs
items.summary: The merger of crypto, decentralized finance and traditional finance is being referred to as the “Great Convergence” at BlackRock.

items.title: Celsius’ Mashinsky gets permanent trading ban in CFTC settlement
items.summary: The US commodities watchdog has settled with Celsius founder Alex Mashinsky, ending the agency’s first-ever case against a crypto lending platform.

items.title: Ether analysts predict another ‘selling wave’ as ETH struggles to overcome $1.7K
items.summary: Exchange inflows, slumping demand and a 31% drop in Ether futures open interest may signal that another wave of selling could hit ETH.

items.title: Ex-Celsius CEO Mashinsky gets U.S. CFTC ban in final resolution with regulator
items.summary: Alexander Mashinsky, the founder of failed crypto lender Celsius, had earlier been imprisoned for fraud and is now formally banned from CFTC registration.
